activities_projects.title: Nested Bracket Complexity
activities_projects.objective: Master the hierarchy of operations with multiple grouping symbols
steps: Solve the expression: $-25 + [ 14 - \{ 18 - (15 - \overline{4 - 1}) \} ]$
Show the step-by-step simplification starting from the vinculum (bar bracket)
Create a similar problem for a peer that results in a specific target integer like -1
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Accurately applies BODMAS rules to multi-layered integer expressions
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical flow and correct final result (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Distributive Property Proof
activities_projects.objective: Verify the distributive property over subtraction with negative coefficients
steps: Calculate $a 	imes (b - c)$ and $(a 	imes b) - (a 	imes c)$ independently
Compare the results and explain the behavior of signs when multiplying two negatives versus a negative and a positive
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Proves the distributive property holds for all integers
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct sign management in calculations (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Competitive Exam Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Apply integer operations to marking schemes with negative marking
steps: Design a quiz: +5 marks for correct, -2 for incorrect, and -1 for unattempted
Calculate total scores for 3 students with different performance profiles
Determine the minimum number of correct answers needed to stay above zero if 5 questions are wrong
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Solves real-world weighted average problems using integers
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the multi-step scoring logic (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Pattern of Powers
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the behavior of negative integers with exponents
steps: Calculate $(-1)^n$ for $n=1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6$
Generalize a rule for when the result is positive and when it is negative
Predict the value of $(-1)^{2026}$ and $(-1)^{999}$ based on your rule
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Discovers the relationship between parity (even/odd) and integer signs
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of the generalized rule (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Integer Coordinate Geometry
activities_projects.objective: Calculate distance and midpoint between negative coordinates
steps: Plot point A at $(-4, 3)$ and point B at $(2, -5)$
Calculate the vertical and horizontal distance between the points using subtraction
Find the integer coordinates of the point exactly halfway between them
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies integer subtraction to find distances on a Cartesian plane
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision of plotting and distance calculation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Find the Integers' Puzzle
activities_projects.objective: Solve systems of logic based on integer properties
steps: Find two integers whose product is $-24$ and whose difference is $11$
Find three consecutive integers whose sum is $-15$
Write out the algebraic reasoning used to find the numbers
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops algebraic thinking through integer constraints
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of integer pairs/triplets (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Division Algorithm Verification
activities_projects.objective: Understand the relationship between Dividend, Divisor, Quotient, and Remainder
steps: Divide $-57$ by $4$ and identify the quotient and remainder
Verify the result using the formula: $Dividend = (Divisor 	imes Quotient) + Remainder$
Discuss if the remainder can ever be negative in standard mathematics
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Deepens understanding of the division algorithm with negative values
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct verification of the formula (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Closure Property Critique
activities_projects.objective: Test closure property across all four operations
steps: Provide examples to show integers are closed under addition, subtraction, and multiplication
Find a counter-example to prove integers are NOT closed under division
Explain why the set of integers is insufficient for division (leading to rational numbers)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically evaluates the set of integers as a mathematical system
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of the counter-example provided (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Atmospheric Pressure Project
activities_projects.objective: Model change in pressure using integer rates
steps: Assume pressure drops by $10$ units for every $1000m$ rise
Calculate the total pressure change for a flight climbing from $-200m$ (below sea level) to $11,000m$
Represent the final pressure as an expression relative to sea level
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Models complex linear change using integer multiplication and addition
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the rate-based calculation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Cryptic Integer Code
activities_projects.objective: Use operations to decrypt a message
steps: Assign A=1, B=2... Z=26. Create a message where each letter is hidden by an expression
Example: To get 'C' (3), the code could be $(-15 \div -5)$
Create a 5-letter word code using at least one multiplication and one division per letter
Challenge a friend to decode it
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Reinforces computational fluency with integers through gamification
activities_projects.evaluation: Complexity and correctness of the encoded expressions (10 marks)
